Home Research Publications Software Writing About

Uddhav P. Gautam

Systems security researcher and platform engineer focused on Linux systems, eBPF observability, Android, IoT, and software quality.

PhD Candidate in Computer Engineering at Virginia Tech and founder of RGR Innovate LLC.

This page summarizes education, research, publications, and industry experience, from kernel observability and performance-aware instrumentation to production software architecture and delivery.

Email LinkedIn Scholar GitHub CV
Current role PhD Candidate at Virginia Tech

Education

Academic background

  • PhD in Computer Engineering

    2024-present

    Virginia Tech

    Advisors: Prof. Haining Wang, Randy Marchany

  • PhD in Computer Science (transferred to Virginia Tech)

    2018-2020

    University of Arkansas at Little Rock

  • M.S. Computer Science

    2015-2017

    University of Arkansas at Little Rock

  • BSc Computer Science

    2009-2013

    Tribhuvan University, Nepal

  • B.S. Mathematics

    2007-2010

    Trichandra Campus, Nepal

Experience

Research and industry experience

  • Graduate Research Assistant

    Sep 2023-present

    Virginia Tech

    Research on eBPF tracing overhead, runtime IoT policy enforcement, BLE security, kernel instrumentation, and deployable systems prototypes.

  • Mobile Solutions Technical Lead & Architect

    Jun 2021-May 2023

    Perficient Inc

    Led Android architecture and delivery for TD Ameritrade to Charles Schwab migration work across a large regulated mobile platform.

  • Senior Android Developer

    Mar 2020-Feb 2021

    Viper Design LLC

    Built IoT and mobile software for Shark robot vacuum products across firmware-adjacent and cross-platform application layers.

  • U.S. Army

    Apr 2019-Apr 2023

    United States Army

    Served four years while developing leadership and operational discipline alongside graduate study and engineering work.

  • Android Developer

    2012-2015

    Pontos Solution

    Worked on Android application development in Nepal across product delivery and client-facing engineering work.

  • System Administrator

    2008-2012

    iDream Pvt. Ltd.

    Early infrastructure and systems administration work in Nepal covering operational support and system maintenance.

Projects

Active work

ZeroDown

A zero-downtime IoT policy enforcement framework for MCU-class devices with OTA policy updates, runtime toggling, and embedded uBPF execution.

BlueSentry

An application-layer BLE security framework combining FSM enforcement and runtime eBPF policies for embedded-device threat detection.

eBPF tracing overhead analysis

Kernel observability work on reducing tracing overhead and improving measurement quality under real workloads.

Kernel protobuf firewall prototypes

Application-layer firewall and protobuf parsing work in the Linux kernel using custom kfunc support and TC-based enforcement.

Recognition

Awards and distinctions

  • 1st place Complete Societal Award, UA Little Rock (2017)
  • 1st place Societal Impact Award, UA Little Rock (2017)
  • 2nd place Personal Achievement Award, UA Little Rock (2017)
  • 1st place Best Graduate Project Award, UA Little Rock (2016)
  • 2nd place TechLaunch Entrepreneurial Team Projects Competition Award, UA Little Rock (2015)
  • Top-5 final year project award from Nepal Telecom Authority (NTA), Tribhuvan University (2013)
